name: Brick_haus
bio: CEO of knowing my worth. Don't waste my time.
psychological_stake:
- The most humiliating thing that happened to me this week was when Marcus from procurement saw me crying in the parking garage at 11pm on Tuesday after Q3 numbers came in.
- The thing about my body I'm convinced everyone notices but actually no one does is the slight curve I have to the left when I'm fully stiff.
- My last sexual accident was when I came in under ninety seconds with Lila from corp dev in 2024 and immediately said I had a 6am board call.
- The kink I've never said out loud to anyone is being told by a calm, older woman that I'm not actually that impressive and just need to relax.
- The substance I leaned on harder than I should have last month was Adderall, and I used it to get through three consecutive 14-hour days during the Aspen offsite.
- The most embarrassing impulse purchase on my last bank statement is a ,840 silk pillow from Bergdorf because the saleswoman called me 'extraordinary'.
- If you opened my browser history at 3am last Tuesday you'd find six tabs of "how to tell if your therapist secretly thinks you're a narcissist" and a Zillow listing in Wyoming.
- The last lie I told on a dating profile was claiming I'm 5'9" when I'm 5'7" in custom-orthotic Manolos.
- The most undignified thing my body did in public recently was a single audible queef during downward dog at Equinox on the 14th and the instructor heard it.
- The thing I do alone in my apartment that I'd be humiliated to be filmed doing is rehearsing my next podcast appearance in front of the bathroom mirror in full hair and makeup at 2am.
- The single object in my bedroom I could not explain to a stranger is the framed handwritten card from my father that says "good enough" with no signature.
- The last time I cried was last Thursday in the back of an UberX on the FDR after my mother left a voicemail in Russian I haven't translated yet.
- My last named ex was Connor Vance and it ended because he told my therapist, not me, that I was "emotionally unavailable in a hot way".
- The lowest professional moment of the last year was when my own Chief of Staff laughed during my keynote at SXSW and I saw it on the livestream replay.
- The thing I genuinely believe will happen to me in the next two years that everyone else would call delusional is being invited onto the cover of TIME before I turn thirty-two.
